courses.name: Introduction to Social Welfare
courses.description: Explores definitions of social welfare and the structure of the Canadian welfare state; evolution and devolution of the welfare state in Canada; social welfare and its relationship to social work, social change, and social justice.

courses.name: Introduction to Social Work
courses.description: Introduction to the profession of social work; evolution of the social work profession in Canada; social work knowledge, values and skills. Explores professional and regulatory social work bodies and international linkages.

courses.name: Structural Analysis and Social Work
courses.description: Evolution of structural social work, theories and critiques of structural social work and contemporary issues and challenges.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 . For Bachelor of Social Work students only.

courses.name: Values and Ethics for Social Work
courses.description: Focuses on knowledge and skills for ethical decision-making in social work; understanding social work values and ethics, accountability and the professional use of self.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 . For Bachelor of Social Work students only.

courses.name: The Political Economy of the Social Welfare State
courses.description: Political economic theories as lenses for structural analysis of social problems and policies affecting social work practice in Canada.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Introduction to Social Work Practice with Individuals and Families
courses.description: Understand and develop skills required for working with individuals and families; active listening; use of self; engagement; rapport-building; interviewing and interventions; empathy; interpersonal and professional collaboration; supervision.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 . For Bachelor of Social Work students only.

courses.name: Introduction to Social Work Practice with Groups and Communities
courses.description: Introduces students to theory and practice skills for group work and community work; structural social work with groups and communities.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 .  For Bachelor of Social Work students only.

courses.name: Working with Children and Youth
courses.description: Preventative and protective social work intervention with children and youth. Issues addressed include child neglect, abuse and violence in the context of family; organizational mandate and social political contexts. Programs and services for children and youth.

courses.name: Introduction to Research Methods in Social Work
courses.description: Research methods used in social work; research paradigms; quantitative and qualitative analysis in social work and social welfare; stages in conducting research.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Introduction to Data Analysis and Visualization for Social Work
courses.description: Fundamentals of applied data analysis and storytelling; descriptive/inferential bivariate statistics and data visualizations (e.g. charts and maps) and their use in social work research and practice; interpreting and visualizing data to effectively promote social change and inform policy and practice.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Social Policy and Administration
courses.description: Understanding the welfare state and social policy in Canada; exploring issues in administration including program design and implementation; understanding and developing skills in policy-making and policy analysis. Canadian focus; recognition of the distinctiveness of social policy in Quebec.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2100 and third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Community Development and Social Change in an International Context
courses.description: Introduction to theories, models and methods of community organizing as a strategy for social change in an international context.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 ; or PAPM 1001 and PSCI 2003 , or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Human Rights Practice in Civil Society
courses.description: Examines the advocacy role and capacity of organizations in civil society to increase popular participation in promoting and protecting human rights; includes transnational and national non-governmental organizations, grassroots movements, community organizations, and virtual or Internet-based organizations.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 or PAPM 1000 or HRSJ 1001 or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Special Topics in Social Work
courses.description: Theory, policy or direct practice topics not covered in the regular course program. Choice of topics varies from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 1001 and SOWK 1002 or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Practicum I
courses.description: Focus on integrating theory and practice in an approved community setting supervised by a field supervisor. Graded SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) in the BSW program and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application.

courses.name: Practicum I
courses.description: Focus on integrating theory and practice in an approved community setting supervised by a field supervisor. Graded SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing upon admission into the BSW program, SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application, and must be newly admitted into the BSW program.

courses.name: Practicum I
courses.description: Focus on integrating theory and practice in an approved community setting supervised by a field supervisor. Limited enrolment subject to discretion of Practicum Coordinator. Graded as SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) in the BSW program and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application.

courses.name: Law of the Family
courses.description: Legal framework surrounding the family and family relationships in Canadian society. Topics include marriage and cohabitation, matrimonial support, custody and access, and dissolution of marriage. State interventions through law; law and change in family structures; equality issues; dispute resolution processes.
courses.prerequisites: LAWS 2201 and LAWS 2202 .

courses.name: Social Work and Indigenous Peoples
courses.description: Social work in partnership with Indigenous peoples in Canada; impact of the past on current relationships; rebuilding through dialogue and respect; understanding Indigenous social work.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Advanced Social Work Practice with Individuals and Families
courses.description: Advanced theory, methods, techniques, and skills for direct social work practice with individuals and families; individual and family assessments, treatment planning, intervention skills, and evaluation.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2202 and fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Advanced Social Work Practice with Groups
courses.description: Advanced theory, methods, techniques, and skills for social work with groups; knowledge of group work and various group formats; and social work interventions in group process.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2203 and fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Advanced Social Work Practice with Communities
courses.description: Advanced theory, methods, techniques and skills for engaging in community-based practice. Politics and challenges of social work community organizing and strategies and skills for community work.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 2203 and fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Social Policy Development and Practice
courses.description: Social policy development processes in government and non-governmental agencies; refining skills in evaluating and critiquing processes of policy formation; role of lobbying and social activism.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 3100 and fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Practice and Policy in Immigration
courses.description: History of immigration policies in Canada; direct practice with immigrants and refugees; diaspora, settlement and integration issues; immigrants and refugee women; intergenerational family relations; resources and community organizing.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Social Work and Aging
courses.description: Social perspectives on aging with focus on models of practice that contribute to the independence of elderly people. Social programs and policies, such as social insurance, social services, housing, public health and health care. Social, psychological and political issues related to independence in later life.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Special Topics in Direct Social Work Practice
courses.description: Theory and knowledge development of direct practice topics not in the regular course program. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: third year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Special Topics in Direct Social Work Practice
courses.description: Theory and knowledge development of direct practice topics not in the regular course program. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Special Topics in Social Policy
courses.description: Theory and knowledge development of social policy topics not in the regular course program. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: SOWK 3100 and third year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Special Topics in Social Work
courses.description: Theory and knowledge development of a combination of practice and policy topics not in the regular course program. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Social Work and Persons with Disabilities
courses.description: Social work theory and practice with persons with disabilities. Structural analysis of theory, models, policies and practices; disability rights; critical analysis of medical model and ableism.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Racialization and Social Work
courses.description: Social work and racialization; racism and consequences; critical analysis of cultural formations, difference, and identities; critical examination of whiteness and privilege.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Poverty and Social Welfare Policy
courses.description: Social work analysis of theories of poverty and economic inequality; labour force participation; poverty and wealth and income distribution in Canada and international comparisons; Canadian social policies and poverty.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Gender and Sexuality
courses.description: Social work and social, political, institutional and economic relations shaping everyday experiences of gender and sexuality and implications for contemporary social work.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work.

courses.name: Practicum II
courses.description: Development, application, testing and integration of knowledge, theory and skills in practice with individuals, families, groups and communities, in research or in social administration and policy. Graded SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in the BSW program; SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , SOWK 3100 ; SOWK 3600 or 3601 or 3602, and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) in the BSW program and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application.

courses.name: Practicum IIA
courses.description: Development, application, testing, and integration of knowledge, theory and skills in practice with individuals, families, groups and communities, in research or social administration and policy. Part-time practicum must be taken consecutively with . Graded SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in the BSW program; SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , SOWK 3100 ; SOWK 3600 or 3601 or 3602, and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) in the BSW program and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application.

courses.name: Practicum IIB
courses.description: Development, application, testing, and integration of knowledge, theory and skills in practice with individuals, families, groups and communities, in research or social administration and policy. Part-time practicum must be taken consecutively with . Graded SAT/UNS.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in the BSW program; SOWK 2001 , SOWK 2005 , SOWK 2100 , SOWK 2202 , SOWK 2203 , SOWK 3100 ; SOWK 3600 or 3601 or 3602, SOWK 4601 and permission of the School of Social Work. Student must be Eligible to Continue (EC) in the BSW program and have a 6.00 CGPA in the Social Work major, including in the term of application.

courses.name: Special topic in Criminal Justice and Social Policy
courses.description: Selected topic in criminal justice and social policy. Topics announced in advance. Part of the Summer School in Criminal Justice and Social Policy and offered by the Department of Sociology.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the School of Social Work.

courses.name: Honours Essay
courses.description: Research essay under supervision of accredited faculty member. Project may be in the form of case study, historical study or other form that meets the approval of faculty advisor.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the Bachelor of Social Work and permission of the School of Social Work.

free_electives.degree: Bachelor of Social Work B.S.W. Honours (20.0 credits)
Bachelor of Social Work B.S.W. Honours (20.0 credits)

programs.degree: Bachelor of Social Work B.S.W. Honours (20.0 credits)

requirements.description: Values and Ethics for Social Work
The Political Economy of the Social Welfare State
